quotes.text: The best among you (Muslims) are those who learn the Qur'an and teach it.
Whoever recites a letter from the Book of Allah will receive one good deed worth ten times its value.
The Quran is a healing for what is in the hearts.
Indeed, this Qur'an guides to that which is most suitable.
And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
Recite the Quran for it will come as an intercessor for its reciters on the Day of Resurrection.
The one who is skilled in the Quran will be with the honourable, righteous scribes.
It will be said to the companion of the Quran: Recite and rise in status.
The heart that has no Quran in it is like a ruined house.
Hold fast to the Quran, for it is the rope of Allah extended to you from the heaven to the earth.

tips: Connect verses conceptually. Don't just memorize sounds — try to understand the flow and logical progression.
Start after Fajr. Your mind is sharpest before the distractions of the day begin.
Recite aloud. Hearing your own voice reinforces neural pathways for retention.
Review before sleeping. The brain consolidates memory during sleep.
Write out verses by hand to engage multiple senses in memorization.

howTo.title: How to memorize a page
howTo.intro: Use the Listen button on a page to open the reader, where you can listen and read the tafsir; come back and mark the page done when you can recite it.

steps.title: Listen first
steps.body: Play the page from a reciter a few times while following along with the text, to get the correct pronunciation (tajweed) into your ear.

steps.title: Understand it
steps.body: Read the tafsir so the words carry meaning, not just sound.

steps.title: Break it up
steps.body: Memorize a few lines (or one ayah) at a time, never the whole page at once.

steps.title: Repeat aloud
steps.body: Say each small piece out loud until it flows smoothly.

steps.title: Test yourself
steps.body: Cover the text and recite the piece from memory; peek only to fix a slip, then test again. This self-testing is the most important step for long-term memory.

steps.title: Connect
steps.body: Join the pieces together, and link the start of the new page to the end of the previous one.

steps.title: Recite & mark done
steps.body: Recite the whole page from memory, then mark it memorized. Tip: review it again before sleep and after Fajr — the app then schedules it for spaced review.

settings.cycleReviewHint: How many pages to review each day

settings.modeFixedDesc: Set an exact page count yourself.
settings.juzGridNote: Orange = partially memorized (kept by default). Click to select all → click again to mark for removal (red) → click once more to restore.
settings.rangeTabHint: Your current memorized pages as ranges. Remove a row to exclude those pages, or add new rows to include more.
settings.preservedNote: + {{count}} pages from partially memorized items will also be kept
settings.cycleStartDesc: Reviews cycle through your memorized pages in order from this point, then wrap around. Useful when you want to focus on a specific section first.
settings.cycleStartPageInvalid: Must be a page you have memorized
settings.syncTabNote: Selections are synced when you switch tabs

features.title: Designed for Deep Focus
features.subtitle: Everything you need to read, memorize, review, and retain the Quran
features.libraryDesc: More than a tracker — a complete mushaf at your fingertips. Read every page, listen to renowned reciters, and study the meaning of each verse, all in one place.
features.libraryReadDesc: Browse all 604 pages in authentic Uthmani script with full tashkeel.

features.reviewDesc: Spaced repetition algorithms ensure you review right before you forget.
features.progressDesc: Visualize your journey through all 30 Juz. See exactly how much you've accomplished and how far you have to go.
features.motivateDesc: Stay motivated with daily streaks and unlock badges for milestones like finishing a Surah or a Juz.
features.bilingualDesc: Use the entire app in fluent Arabic with full right-to-left layout, or in English — and switch between a calm light theme and a restful dark mode whenever you like.

values.title: Why Our Platform
values.bilingual: Works fully in Arabic and English

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Get started in minutes and build a lasting Hifz habit
howItWorks.step1Desc: Register and tell us how much you've already memorized and how many pages per day you can commit to.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Each day we assign new pages to memorize and review old ones using spaced repetition.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Check off tasks, maintain your streak, and watch your progress grow across all 30 Juz.

items.q: Is Quran Tracker free to use?
items.a: Yes, completely free. All core features — personalized plans, spaced repetition reviews, and progress tracking — are available at no cost.

items.q: How does the smart review system work?
items.a: Pages are scheduled for revision based on how recently you memorized them. Newer pages appear more often, with intervals growing as your retention strengthens — grounded in the Ebbinghaus Forgetting Curve.

items.q: Can I track specific Surahs or page ranges?
items.a: Yes. During onboarding and in Settings you can select memorized content by Juz, by Surah, or by custom page ranges.

items.q: What happens if I miss a day?
items.a: No penalties. Your daily plan stays the same — missed review pages simply join the next cycle. Consistency matters more than perfection.

footer.copyright: © 2025 Quran Tracker. Dedicated to the pursuit of Hifz.

about.title: About Quran Tracker
about.subtitle: A digital companion for your Hifz journey
about.missionText: Quran Tracker is built to help every Muslim who has ever felt overwhelmed by the task of memorizing the Quran. By combining the classical Islamic tradition of Muraja'ah (مراجعة) with modern cognitive science, we make daily Hifz sustainable, measurable, and spiritually fulfilling.
about.scienceText: The review system is based on Ebbinghaus's Forgetting Curve (1885) and spaced repetition (Pimsleur 1967, Leitner 1972): review intervals grow as retention strengthens. The Islamic concept of Muraja'ah — systematic revision — is the spiritual root of what this app does digitally.
about.step1Desc: Tell us what you've already memorized and your daily page goal.
about.step2Desc: New pages to memorize + spaced review of previously memorized pages.
about.step3Desc: 30-Juz grid, streak tracking, and achievement badges celebrate your journey.
about.projectText: This application was developed as a graduation project. It implements functional and non-functional requirements including Arabic UI, responsive design, and spaced repetition.
about.contactText: Have feedback or questions? We'd love to hear from you:
